content.problemStatement:
**This is an interactive problem.**

Chef has a secret integer. The only information you have is that it has an odd number of factors and that it lies between 111 and 10610^6106 inclusive.

You have been challenged by Chef to find his number. You may make up to 2,0002,0002,000 guesses (queries). Whenever you guess Chef's number, he only tells you if you guessed correctly or not. Can you find Chef's number?

Interaction
First, you should read a line containing a single integer TTT ― the number of test cases. The description of interaction for TTT test cases follows.
For each test case, you should start by asking queries.

To ask a query, you should print a line containing an integer between 111 and 10610^6106 inclusive ― your guess.
Then, you should read a line containing a single integer: 111 if you correctly guessed Chef's secret number, 000 if your guess was incorrect or −1-1−1 if your guess is invalid (in particular, smaller than 111 or greater than 10610^6106) or you have asked more than 2,0002,0002,000 queries.


As soon as you have correctly guessed Chef's secret number, you should stop asking queries and continue solving the remaining test cases.
If you read −1-1−1, you must immediately terminate your program to receive a Wrong Answer verdict. Otherwise, you may receive any verdict. Don't forget to flush the output after printing each line! You can read more about interactive problems here.
constraints: Chef's secret number lies between 111 and 10610^6106 inclusive
Chef's secret number has an odd number of factors
You ask Chef if 232232232 is his secret number. Chef replies with 000, so it is not.
You ask Chef if 92,73992,73992,739 is his secret number. Chef replies with 000, so it is not.
You ask Chef if 100100100 is his secret number. Chef replies with 111, so this is indeed his secret number.
